LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FREMONT HOMES AND LOANS, INC., MIKE CAI'S GENERLA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF LIZA M. DIGDIGAN. DEFENDANTS FREMONT HOMES AND LOANS INC. AND MIKE CAI'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND FIFTH THROUGH EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE, VIOLATION OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW, NEGLIGENCE, SLANDER OF TITLE AND QUIET TITLE IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE QUIET TITLE CLAIM AGAINST MR. CAI AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF LIZ DIGDIGAN ACKNOWLEDGES THAT HER QUIET TITLE CLAIM DOES NOT LIE AGAINST MR. CAI. EXCEPT FOR HER QUIET TITLE CLAIM AGAINST MR. CAI, ALL CLAIMS ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. LIABILITY AGAINST MR. CAI IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED BASED ON MR. CAI ALLEGEDLY BEING THE ALTER EGO OF FREMONT HOMES. LIABILITY AGAINST FREMONT HOMES IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED BASED ON FREMONT HOMES ALLEGEDLY BEING TOLD PRIOR TO ITS PURCHASE OF MS. DIGDIGAN'S FORMER HOME THAT THE SALE WAS UNLAWFUL AND SHOULD NOT PROCEED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF LIZA DIGDIGAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-18-661838 WITH CASE CGC-18-569224 IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS CONSOLIDATED WITH THE CIVIL ACTION FOR PURPOSES OF TRIAL AND DISCOVERY. UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION TO PROCEED ON THE GENERAL CIVIL ACTION TIMELINE. HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS PAYMENT OF TAXES, INSURANCE, AND MAINTENANCE OF THE PROPERTY FOR THE DURATION OF THE LITIGATION. COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER PRIOR TO THE HEARING TO ADDRESS THESE ISSUES AND PROVIDE THE COURT WITH THE SPECIFIC NUMBERS (E.G. TAXES AND INSURANCE PAID BY DIGDIGAN WHEN SHE WAS THE TITLE OWNER; TAXES AND INSURANCE PAID NOW, ETC.) AND A WORKING PLAN TO PROCEED WHILE FREEMONT HOLDS LEGAL TITLE AND DIGDIGAN IS IN POSSESSION. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). BOTH SIDES WAIVE NOTICE. CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-18-661838 WITH CASE CGC-18-569224
Off Calendar Jan-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
David Martha, 510.332.3943 for Defendant/Opposing party Fremont Homes and Loean Inc.
Nelson Goodell, 415.495.3950 for Plaintiff/Moving party Liza Digdigan.

Discussed. prior to the calling of the case counsel for parties agreed that Plaintiff will pay the HOA dues, the original mortgage payment, taxes and maintenance costs related to the unit. Defendant's counsel requests a stay and no consolidation. Plaintiff's counsel opposes a stay.

The Court rules as follows: PLAINTIFF LIZA DIGDIGAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-18-661838 WITH CASE CGC-18-569224 is GRANTED as follows: Unlawful detainer action is consolidated with the Civil Action for purposes of trial and discovery. Unlawful detainer action to proceed on the general civil action timeline. Hearing Required to address payment of taxes, insurance, and maintenance of the property for the duration of the litigation. Counsel to meet and confer prior to the hearing to address these issues and provide the Court with the specific numbers (e.g. taxes and insurance paid by Digdigan when she was the title owner; taxes and insurance paid now, etc.) and a working plan to proceed while Freemont holds legal title and Digdigan is in possession. Plaintiff to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Both sides waive notice. Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-11-2019 Continued To Mar-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FREMONT HOMES AND LOANS, INC., MIKE CAI's GENERLA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF LIZA M. DIGDIGAN.

Defendants Fremont Homes and Loans Inc. and Mike Cai's demurrer to the first and fifth through eighth causes of action for wrongful foreclosure, violation of the Unfair Competition Law, negligence, slander of title and quiet title is sustained as to the quiet title claim against Mr. Cai and overruled as to all other claims. Plaintiff Liz Digdigan acknowledges that her quiet title claim does not lie against Mr. Cai. Except for her quiet title claim against Mr. Cai, all claims are sufficiently alleged. Liability against Mr. Cai is adequately alleged based on Mr. Cai allegedly being the alter ego of Fremont Homes. Liability against Fremont Homes is adequately alleged based on Fremont Homes allegedly being told prior to its purchase of Ms. Digdigan's former home that the sale was unlawful and should not proceed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
